jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer (COO) – Construction & Home Services
jobs.description:
**About Green Attic**

Green Attic is a growing home\-services company serving residential and commercial customers throughout the Chicagoland area.

Our operations include roofing, insulation, energy\-efficiency improvements, remodeling, restoration, and multi\-trade construction projects.

Ownership has built a strong sales pipeline and revenue engine. We are now hiring an experienced Chief Operating Officer to lead company\-wide execution, strengthen management, improve profitability, and build the systems required for continued growth.

This is not primarily a field\-supervisor or production\-manager position. The COO must understand construction operations deeply, but the main responsibility is to lead the business at an executive level.

The right person will translate company goals into operating plans, develop department leaders, manage financial and operational performance, and ensure the organization executes without depending on ownership for routine decisions.

**Position Overview**

The Chief Operating Officer will oversee day\-to\-day business operations across production, project management, scheduling, customer service, warehouse, purchasing, administration, and field execution.

This leader will work directly with ownership to establish priorities, allocate resources, improve organizational structure, and maintain accountability across departments.

The COO must have significant construction or home\-services experience and understand how projects move from sales and estimating through scheduling, production, billing, completion, warranty, and customer closeout.

This position requires executive judgment, financial discipline, leadership maturity, and the ability to build a scalable operating system across multiple brands and service divisions.

**Executive Responsibilities**

* Translate ownership’s vision and growth goals into clear operating plans, priorities, budgets, and measurable targets.
* Lead company\-wide operations across roofing, insulation, remodeling, construction, customer service, scheduling, warehouse, and administrative functions.
* Develop and manage department leaders, project managers, production managers, supervisors, and support teams.
* Establish clear organizational structure, decision\-making authority, reporting relationships, and accountability.
* Own operational performance, including revenue execution, gross margin, labor efficiency, project completion, customer satisfaction, and cash\-flow coordination.
* Review financial and operational reports to identify risks, missed targets, waste, and growth opportunities.
* Build annual, quarterly, and weekly operating plans with clear owners, deadlines, and scorecards.
* Create a consistent management rhythm using leadership meetings, KPI reviews, departmental scorecards, issue tracking, and documented follow\-up.
* Ensure sales commitments are properly transferred into estimating, scheduling, production, billing, and project completion.
* Improve communication and coordination between sales, production, project management, warehouse, accounting, and customer service.
* Identify leadership gaps and recruit, train, coach, or replace team members when required.
* Establish performance expectations and hold managers accountable for results, not activity or excuses.
* Lead major operational decisions involving staffing, capacity, scheduling, subcontractors, equipment, materials, vendors, and expansion.
* Reduce operational dependence on ownership by creating reliable leaders, systems, and escalation procedures.
* Evaluate new services, markets, technologies, and operational investments based on profitability and execution capacity.
* Protect the company from operational, safety, compliance, customer\-service, and financial risk.

**Construction and Production Oversight**

The COO will not personally manage every project or crew. However, this person must understand construction well enough to challenge managers, identify weak planning, evaluate risk, and determine whether operations are being run correctly.

Responsibilities include:

* Oversee production performance across roofing, insulation, remodeling, restoration, and multi\-trade projects.
* Ensure project scopes are reviewed for completeness, constructability, labor needs, material needs, scheduling risks, and margin protection.
* Confirm that departments have proper systems for job readiness, crew assignments, material coordination, change orders, documentation, quality control, and closeout.
* Review major job delays, callbacks, warranty issues, safety incidents, customer disputes, and margin losses.
* Visit active projects as needed to verify management reports and assess operational performance.
* Ensure field and production leaders maintain quality, safety, cleanliness, documentation, and customer communication standards.
* Identify recurring field problems and require permanent process corrections rather than repeated emergency fixes.

**Financial and Performance Management**

* Own and improve operational profitability across all service divisions.
* Partner with ownership and accounting to establish budgets, forecasts, labor targets, and margin expectations.
* Review project\-level and departmental financial performance.
* Track gross margin, labor cost, material cost, subcontractor cost, callbacks, warranty expenses, write\-offs, and outstanding receivables.
* Identify unprofitable services, crews, workflows, vendors, or project types and implement corrective action.
* Improve job costing, purchasing controls, change\-order management, billing readiness, and project closeout.
* Ensure managers understand how their decisions affect company profitability and cash flow.
* Build scorecards that provide ownership with a clear view of performance without requiring constant operational involvement.

**Systems and Organizational Development**

* Build and improve SOPs, workflows, checklists, approval systems, reporting processes, and accountabi

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
Cyclone Technology LLC

Phoenix Location

Chief Operating Officer

**Chief Operating Officer job description**

Cyclone Technology is a growing company, with a global customer base of airports, municipalities, and surface maintenance contractors. This position will be a successor to a retiring successful on\-site COO position. The position reports to and is generally the right\-hand person to our CEO.

We’re searching for a Chief Operating Officer who has strong leadership skills and the ability to approach business with a creative point of view. The COO will lead our team of high\-performing professionals to be efficient, productive, and effective. The ideal candidate has demonstrated success in a mid\-size manufacturing business, preferably having worked his/her way up to an executive role. Understanding the need for quality controls and effective customer outreach and care is also a critical role of the COO position.

**Objectives of this role**

· Collaborate with CEO in setting and driving organizational vision, operations and products strategy,

· Translate strategy into actionable steps for growth, implementing organization\-wide goal setting, performance management, and annual operations planning

· Oversee company operations and employee productivity, building a highly inclusive culture that ensures teams members can thrive and organizational goals are met

· Implement financial controls and understand the accounting impact and results of operating decisions. Periodic reporting of results to the CEO and board of directors.

· Ensure effective recruiting, onboarding, professional development, performance management, and retention

· Ensure compliance with national and local business regulations, and take appropriate action when necessary

**Responsibilities**

· Implement business strategies and plans that align with the short\-and long\-term objectives developed in tandem with CEO

· Analyze internal operations and identify areas for process enhancement

· Coordinate operations, HR, manufacturing, logistics, purchasing, vendor relations, and personnel and accounting, with sales management so that sufficient investment capital can be budgeted for near\-term growth targets

· Manage capital investments and expenses aggressively to ensure that the company achieves company targets for growth and profitability

· Monitor performance with tracking software and take corrective measures when necessary, and prepare detailed updates and forecasts

· Build and maintain trusting relationships with key suppliers, customers, dealers/distributors, partners, and other stakeholders

**Required skills and qualifications** 

· Five or more years of strong Capital Equipment Manufacturing management background and/ or experience in executive leadership roles

· Demonstrated leadership skills, with steadfast resolve and personal integrity

· Understanding of advanced business finance and planning, and regulatory issues

· Solid grasp of data analysis and performance metrics

· Action orientation: Ability to diagnose problems quickly and foresee potential issues

· Strong communicator

· Ability and desire to travel as required

**Preferred skills and qualifications** 

· **Education**: Bachelor’s in Business Administration, Management, or a related field: a Masters degree or MBA is highly preferred.

· **Experience:** 5\+ years of senior management of executive\-level experience in an operational capacity.

· **Skills**: Strong analytical prowess, proven change leadership, and exceptional critical thinking and problem\-solving abilities.

· **Communication**: Exceptional interpersonal and public speaking skills with the ability to inspire diverse stakeholders and act as a trusted advisor to the CEO

**Compensation:** Salary is commensurate with experience, qualifications, and skills.

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Director of Development
jobs.description:
Northfield Mount Hermon **(NMH)** seeks a strategic and collaborative Director of Development. Reporting to the Chief Advancement Officer (CAO) as their primary strategic partner, the Director of Development (DOD) functions as the department's operational leader, ensuring management continuity and alignment across all advancement initiatives. The position oversees all direct fundraising units including the NMH Fund, parent giving, major gifts for domestic and international supporters, and legacy giving with responsibility for securing financial success of Northfield Mount Hermon. The position manages a team of staff professionals and will work with many volunteers including Alumni and Family Councils, and others as needed both to accomplish the work and to engage constituents in meaningful ways. The position carries an advancement portfolio of 75\+ potential donors to meet institutional goals. **Why NMH**

* Comprehensive insurances, including medical, dental, vision, life, and disability
* A robust 11% 403(b) contribution (with a 3% employee contribution)
* Generous paid time off, including four weeks of annual paid vacation
* Tired of packing lunch? Enjoy meals in our dining hall while on duty and save an average of $2,700 a year.
* Tuition remission for dependent children of high school age who meet the acceptance requirements of the school

**About NMH**


Northfield Mount Hermon, an independent boarding and day school located in the beautiful Connecticut River Valley of Massachusetts, was founded on the principles of inclusivity and community — values that have been central to the school’s mission since 1879\. NMH engages the intellect, compassion, and talents of its students, empowering them to act with humanity and purpose.

**A Day in the Life**


The Director of Development will serve as a strategic partner to the Chief Advancement Officer and a key leader within NMH’s Advancement team. This position will oversee frontline fundraising efforts across annual giving, parent giving, major gifts, and legacy giving while leading a team of advancement professionals and collaborating with a dedicated network of volunteers. In addition to providing strategic leadership for NMH’s comprehensive development program, the Director of Development will cultivate relationships with alumni, parents, and friends through a personal portfolio of major and leadership\-level donors. This role will help shape fundraising strategies, strengthen donor engagement, and build systems that support the continued growth of NMH’s philanthropic community. The successful candidate will be an active member of the NMH community, building relationships across campus and developing a deep understanding of the school’s mission, traditions, and culture.

**Required Skills and Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree required
* 10\+ years of experience leading successful fundraising programs, including experience managing and developing high\-performing teams
* Demonstrated success developing fundraising strategies, cultivating donor relationships, and securing philanthropic support
* Experience managing and engaging volunteers in support of institutional goals
* Strong leadership, project management, and organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a dynamic environment
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to build authentic relationships with a wide range of constituents
* Ability to collaborate effectively across departments while working independently and exercising sound judgment
* Experience using technology platforms and tools, including Google Workspace or Microsoft Office
* Demonstrated discretion, tact, and ability to manage confidential information
* Commitment to working with a diverse and inclusive community and to NMH’s mission and values

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Experience in educational fundraising, particularly within an independent school, college, or university setting
* Advanced degree preferred
* Experience working within a boarding school or residential educational community
* Familiarity with CRM systems such as Slate or other advancement databases


This is a full\-time, on\-site position based at NMH’s campus in Gill, Massachusetts. Frequent travel, along with evening and weekend availability, will be essential for success in this role.

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer (COO) - Behavioral Health & Dual Diagnosis
jobs.description:
Position Summary

We are seeking an experienced, strategic, and growth\-minded Chief Operating Officer (COO) to lead a comprehensive behavioral health and dual diagnosis treatment network in Missouri. This executive will oversee daily operations across residential, detox, intensive outpatient (IOP), and outpatient (OP) programs. The ideal candidate will optimize clinical and administrative workflows, maintain strict regulatory compliance, and scale operations while upholding the highest standards of patient care.

Key Responsibilities

Operational Leadership: Direct all daily administrative, clinical support, facilities, and operational functions across multiple levels of care.

Strategic Growth: Partner with the CEO to execute expansion strategies, open new sites, and scale outpatient services throughout Missouri.

Financial Management: Manage operational budgets, control labor costs, and optimize utilization metrics to drive profitability.

Compliance \& Quality: Ensure continuous compliance with Missouri Department of Mental Health (DMH) regulations, DHSS licensing, and Joint Commission, CARF, and/or State of Missouri accreditation standards.

Clinical Alignment: Collaborate with the Medical Director and Clinical Director to balance top\-tier, evidence\-based care with efficient operational throughput.

Talent Development: Recruit, mentor, and retain a high\-performing leadership team; foster a culture of accountability, empathy, and professional growth.

Data\-Driven Performance: Establish and track key performance indicators (KPIs) related to bed utilization, length of stay, staff retention, and patient outcomes.

Required Qualifications

Education: Master's degree in Healthcare Administration, Business Administration (MBA), Nursing (MSN), or a related behavioral health field preferred. Bachelor's degree with equivalent executive experience required.

Experience: Minimum of 5\-7 years of executive\-level operations experience specifically within behavioral health, addiction treatment, or dual diagnosis facilities.

Continuum Knowledge: Proven experience managing both 24/7 residential facilities and high\-volume outpatient/IOP programs.

Missouri Regulations: Deep familiarity with Missouri DMH certification processes, Medicaid billing workflows, and regional commercial insurance landscapes.

Accreditation Mastery: Documented success leading surveys and accreditation.

Benefits

Highly competitive base salary with performance\-based executive bonus structure.

Generous Paid Time Off (PTO) and executive wellness benefits.

Relocation assistance (if applicable).

We are an equal opportunity employer and prohibit discrimination/harassment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws.

* Relocation assistance

Title: Chief Executive Officer


Stage: Pre\-Seed Series A Healthcare Technology Company

**About Bedrock Talent**
------------------------


At Bedrock Talent, we're intentional about the companies we represent.


We don't partner with every startup that comes our way. We work with founders we'd be excited to work for ourselves, companies solving meaningful problems, and teams we genuinely believe have the potential to build category\-defining businesses.


This is one of those companies.

**About the Company**
---------------------


Our client is an early\-stage healthcare AI company with an ambitious mission: leveraging artificial intelligence, data science, and clinical expertise to improve health outcomes and meaningfully extend healthy human lives.


Backed by an exceptional founding team and world\-class advisors spanning healthcare, technology, AI, and longevity science, the company is building a platform designed to transform complex health data into actionable insights for patients, providers, and researchers.


They're still in the earliest stages of company building, which means this role offers a rare opportunity to shape not only the business, but the future direction of an emerging category.

**The Opportunity**
-------------------


We're looking for a **Chief Executive Officer** who wants to build more than a company—they want to build a legacy.


This is a true 0 1 leadership opportunity for an operator who thrives at the intersection of product, commercialization, fundraising, and company building.


You'll partner with an experienced executive team and renowned advisors to define product\-market fit, establish go\-to\-market strategy, raise capital, recruit exceptional talent, and guide the company from its earliest stages through Series A and beyond.


This role is ideal for someone who enjoys rolling up their sleeves, making decisions with imperfect information, and building organizations from first principles.

**Before You Apply**
--------------------


Rather than bury the important details in a long job description, here are the things that usually determine whether someone gets excited about this opportunity—or realizes it probably isn't the right fit.

### **This Is a True 0 1 CEO Role**


The company has exceptional technical and scientific leadership already in place.


What it needs is a CEO who can connect product to market, vision to execution, and innovation to commercial success.


You'll help define nearly every aspect of how the company grows—from fundraising and hiring to customer acquisition and product strategy.

### **You'll Be the Face of the Company**


You'll serve as the primary storyteller for customers, investors, strategic partners, and future employees.


Success in this role requires someone equally comfortable pitching venture capitalists, meeting with healthcare executives, and recruiting world\-class talent.

This isn't a "fundraising CEO" or a "sales CEO."


The ideal leader understands customers deeply, helps shape product strategy, builds repeatable go\-to\-market motions, and translates technical innovation into real customer value.

### **Mission Comes First**


The company exists to tackle one of humanity's biggest challenges: helping people live healthier, longer lives.


The team is looking for someone who believes meaningful businesses can generate exceptional returns while creating measurable improvements in people's lives.

### **Exceptional Founders and Advisors**


One of the reasons Bedrock Talent chose to partner with this company is the people behind it.


The founders and advisors are thoughtful, ambitious, low ego, and deeply committed to building the right company—not simply the fastest\-growing one.


They're looking for a CEO who shares those values.

**What You'll Do**
------------------

* Define and execute the company's product\-market fit strategy.
* Lead commercialization efforts, including early customer acquisition and strategic partnerships.
* Develop and own the company's go\-to\-market strategy, positioning, and pricing.
* Lead fundraising efforts from pre\-seed through Series A.
* Recruit and build a world\-class leadership team across product, engineering, commercial, and operations.
* Partner closely with technical leadership to ensure product development aligns with customer needs and market demand.
* Represent the company with customers, investors, partners, and the broader healthcare and AI ecosystem.
* Build a mission\-driven culture rooted in ownership, collaboration, and execution.

**We're Looking For Someone Who...**
------------------------------------

* Has successfully built and scaled an early\-stage technology company.
* Has led or played a significant role in venture fundraising.
* Has a proven track record of commercializing innovative products and driving revenue growth.
* Understands healthcare, health technology, or adjacent regulated industries.
* Can engage confidently with engineers, AI researchers, clinicians, and investors alike.
* Has repeatedly attracted exceptional talent into ambitious startup environments.
* Leads with humility, curiosity, and a strong bias toward execution.

-------------------------------------------

* You enjoy operating in ambiguity and creating structure as you scale.
* You believe the best CEOs spend as much time listening as they do leading.
* You're motivated by solving meaningful healthcare problems with lasting societal impact.

jobs.description:
**Director of Advancement**

**About Us** 

Food Share of Lincoln County’s mission is to alleviate hunger in Lincoln County by distributing healthy, nutritious food to our network of agencies and by empowering our community through education. The Food Share warehouse serves as the central distribution point for 23 partner agencies in the county. As a regional food bank, FSLC is overseen by a Board of Directors and collaborates with Oregon Food Bank to distribute USDA commodity foods, food donated by producers in the food industry, and local donations from retailers and individuals.

FSLC has been in existence since September of 1982\. Beyond our longevity, we have strong community goodwill and support, which are significant. Community members make up our volunteers, donors, partner agencies, board of directors, and a variety of businesses and organizations that support our work.

We also are an example of good stewardship and strategy. Our current warehouse/office space is owned outright and we have completed the purchase of our future warehouse location this past year.

FSLC focuses on serving those in the community with respect and dignity and those who are vulnerable; low\-income, working\-class families, military veterans, BIPOC, LGBQT, foster youth, seniors, and unhoused. We partner with fellow non\-profit agencies and housing units to develop programming that requires the fewest obstacles and affords easy access to those in need. We also engage and empower members by offering Nutrition Education regarding fresh foods and healthy eating.

**Position Summary**

The **Director of Advancement** will participate in Food Share’s fundraising and community engagement efforts, focusing on donor relations, events, communications, capital projects, and providing administrative support. This role is ideal for someone who is highly organized, detail\-oriented, and passionate about building community support to address hunger.

**Key Responsibilities**

* **Fundraising \& Donor Relations 50%**
* Maintain donor database; process and acknowledge gifts promptly.
* Support and develop annual giving campaigns, donor stewardship, estate gifts, and cultivation activities.
* Utilize data informed strategies.
* Grant writing, tracking and reporting.
* **Events \& Campaigns 30%**
* Help plan and execute fundraising events and community engagement activities.
* Coordinate logistics, board and volunteer engagement, and follow\-up.
* Assist with capital campaign and other fundraising initiatives.
* **Communications \& Outreach 10%**
* Develop donor\-centered communications, including newsletters, social media posts.
* Share stories of impact and increase visibility of Food Share’s programs.
* **Administrative Support 10%**
* Provide admin and support as needed.

**Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in communications, nonprofit management, or related field; or equivalent work experience.
* 2 \- 3 years of nonprofit development, communications, or administrative experience preferred.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills.
* Knowledge using donor management software (e.g., Little Green Light, DonorPerfect, or similar).
* Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite, Canva, and social media platforms.
* Highly organized, detail\-oriented, and able to manage multiple priorities.
* Passion for hunger relief and commitment to Food Share’s mission.

**Compensation \& Benefits**

* Salary: $24\.00 \- $26\.00 per hour \- Paid health, vision, dental plan. After one year, matching up to 3% retirement plan.
* Benefits include health insurance, paid vacation, sick leave, and a retirement plan.
* Professional development opportunities.

Pay: From $24\.00 per hour

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance

ABOUT CLEVELAND PLAY HOUSE



Founded in 1915, Cleveland Play House (CPH) is America’s first professional regional theatre and a nationally recognized cultural institution. Throughout its history, CPH has launched groundbreaking new work, produced celebrated classics and contemporary plays, and served as a vital artistic home for generations of artists and audiences.

  



As Cleveland Play House enters a transformative new chapter, the organization is building on its legacy while embracing new opportunities for artistic excellence, community impact, and long\-term sustainability. The Director of Development will play a critical leadership role in shaping that future.

Reporting directly to the Managing Director and serving as a member of the senior leadership team, the Director of Development will cultivate meaningful relationships with donors, foundations, corporations, and community partners while developing strategies to increase contributed revenue and deepen philanthropic engagement.

* Work with grant\-writing support to ensure timely submission of proposals and reports.
* Identify new institutional funding opportunities aligned with organizational priorities.



Annual Giving and Donor Stewardship


* Provide strategic oversight of annual giving programs and donor communications.
* Ensure thoughtful and meaningful stewardship experiences across donor levels.
* Collaborate with Marketing and Communications staff to develop fundraising messaging and donor\-facing content.
* Strengthen donor retention, renewal, and upgrade strategies.



Board and Volunteer Engagement


* Partner with the Managing Director and Board leadership to advance fundraising efforts.
* Support Development Committee activities and fundraising initiatives.
* Assist trustees in identifying, cultivating, and soliciting prospective donors.
* + Help foster a culture of philanthropy throughout the organization.



Team Leadership


* Supervise and mentor development staff.
* Establish clear goals, priorities, and accountability measures.
* Foster a collaborative, donor\-centered, and mission\-driven culture.
* Support staff professional growth and development.



Organizational Leadership


* Serve as an active member of the senior leadership team.
* Participate in institutional planning and cross\-departmental collaboration.
* Represent Cleveland Play House at community, donor, and civic events.
* Support organizational initiatives that advance audience engagement, community relationships, and institutional visibility.

  



QUALIFICATIONS



Required Qualifications


* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent combination of education and experience.
* Minimum of five years of progressively responsible fundraising experience.
* Demonstrated success securing six\-and seven\-figure major gifts.
* Experience developing and implementing fundraising strategies that achieve measurable results.
* Strong relationship\-building and donor cultivation skills.
* Excellent written, verbal, and presentation abilities.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively in a fast\-paced environment.
* Strong organizational and project management skills.
* Ability to work evenings and weekends as required for donor events, performances, and organizational activities.



Preferred Qualifications


* Seven or more years of fundraising leadership experience.
* Experience in performing arts, higher education, cultural, or nonprofit organizations.
* Experience with campaign planning and execution.
* Knowledge of Cleveland’s philanthropic community.
* Experience managing fundraising staff and volunteers.

jobs.title: Senior Director of Advancement
jobs.description:
**Supervisor:** Executive Director

**Supervising:** Manager of Grants and Foundations, Development Associate, Digital Systems Specialist (consultant)

**Status:** Full\-time (40 hours), Exempt, Hybrid

**About the Organization:**

Family Nurturing Center of Massachusetts (FNC) was founded in 1994 with the mission to advance the healthy development of children, caregivers, and communities through parenting education, early childhood engagement, coalition building, and training for providers. For more than 30 years, FNC has provided free family engagement programs in Boston's underserved neighborhoods, with recent expansion to Chelsea, MA, and offered nationally recognized training and professional development services.

Each year, FNC engages over 8,500 children, caregivers, and professionals. Programs are offered in multiple languages and are grounded in values of equity, collaboration, and family leadership.

In 2020, FNC opened a 12,800\-square\-foot headquarters in Dorchester, expanding its capacity to serve families and strengthen community partnerships. With a staff of 72 (full and part\-time), a committed cross\-sector board of directors, and an annual operating budget of $6M, FNC is poised for continued impact.

**About the Opportunity:**

The Senior Director of Advancement serves as a member of Family Nurturing Center's Executive Leadership Team and as the Executive Director's strategic partner for philanthropy, communications, and external engagement. This executive leadership role provides strategic leadership for developing and executing a comprehensive resource development strategy that advances FNC's mission, strengthens long\-term financial sustainability, and expands the organization's impact across Massachusetts.

Working with the Development Team (Manager of Grants and Foundations, Development \& Communications Associate, Digital Systems Specialist) the Senior Director of Advancement has ultimate responsibility for individual giving, institutional philanthropy, public funding strategy, marketing and communications, donor engagement, and community partnerships. This role also partners closely with the Senior Director of Programs \& Impact to identify emerging community needs, shape funding priorities, and pursue public and private funding opportunities that deepen FNC's impact.

This leader will inherit a strong foundation of philanthropic relationships, organizational credibility, and community trust while playing a pivotal role in shaping the next chapter of FNC's advancement strategy during an exciting period of organizational growth and leadership transition.

**Principal Duties and Responsibilities:**

Advancement Strategy \& Resource Development

* Serve as a member of the Executive Leadership Team, contributing to organizational strategy, annual planning, budgeting, and enterprise\-wide decision\-making.
* Partner with the Executive Director to develop and execute FNC's comprehensive resource development strategy in support of the organization's mission, strategic priorities, and long\-term financial sustainability.
* Advise the Executive Director on fundraising strategy, philanthropic trends, external partnerships, and opportunities to diversify organizational revenue.
* Partner with the Executive Director and Board leadership to strengthen Board engagement in philanthropy, stewardship, and resource development.
* Represent FNC with donors, foundations, corporate partners, government agencies, and community stakeholders while serving as an ambassador for the organization's mission and values.

Leadership and Resource Development:

* Lead the development and execution of a comprehensive resource development strategy that integrates individual giving, institutional philanthropy, public funding, corporate partnerships, events, and communications.
* Develop and personally manage a portfolio of major donors and prospects while partnering with the Executive Director and Board to expand philanthropic support.
* Execute annual fundraising planning and execution to achieve organizational revenue goals established in partnership with the Executive Director and Board of Directors.
* Identify opportunities to diversify revenue and strengthen FNC's long\-term financial sustainability.
* Evaluate fundraising performance and recommend strategic adjustments based on organizational priorities, fundraising results, and emerging opportunities.
* Provide strategic oversight of institutional fundraising, partnering with the Manager of Grants \& Foundations to ensure high\-quality proposal development, strong funder stewardship, and continuous improvement of FNC's institutional fundraising strategy.
* Partner with the Senior Director of Programs \& Impact and Director of Finance \& Administration to identify and pursue public grants, government contracts, and strategic funding opportunities that strengthen organizational impact and financial sustainability.
* Co\-lead the development of competitive public grant and contract proposals, ensuring alignment between organizational priorities, programmatic needs, and funding opportunities.

**Advancement Operations:**

* Supervise the Development \& Communications Associate and Manager of Grants \& Foundations.
* Provide strategic oversight of advancement systems, donor stewardship processes, CRM utilization, digital fundraising, consultant relationships, and data integrity.
* Lead systems improvements that strengthen fundraising operations, donor engagement, reporting, and organizational efficiency.
* Ensure effective management of advancement data, gift processing, internal reconciliations, fundraising analytics, and related operational processes.
* Evaluate future Advancement staffing needs and make recommendations as organizational capacity and resources evolve.

**Board and Donor Engagement:**

* Convene, steward, and support the Development Committee of the Board of Directors.
* Partner with Board leadership to strengthen Board engage

jobs.title: Associate Director of Philanthropy
jobs.description:
### **We’re Looking for You:**

Have you ever asked yourself, “How can I make a difference?”, but you don’t know where to go or have the time to do it? Welcome to The Nature Conservancy.


You have found the solution and now you can pointedly make a difference every day! By joining our Kentucky team, you will become part of a group that is a force for nature and people. Our team consists of a dedicated group who are leading the conservation effort on multiple fronts and who expertly bring strategy and action together. We’re looking for an individual with fundraising or business development experience who wants to make a difference. The Conservancy is a global conservation leader and one of the top fundraising charities in the United States.


### **What We Can Achieve Together:**

The Associate Director of Philanthropy (ADOP) works closely with the philanthropy team to implement and coordinate effective multi\-year strategies and plans for gift prospects, including corporate, foundation, and individual donors. TNC is embarking on its largest fundraising campaign to date in support of our 2030 goals. From reforestation, land protection, and fire management in eastern Kentucky to regenerative agriculture and wetland restoration in western Kentucky, The Nature Conservancy is building a brighter future and boosting resiliency for nature and people in the Commonwealth.


The ADOP is responsible for building a portfolio of qualified donors and developing relationships. Relationship\-based fundraising is our approach, and they will positively connect and engage with their teammates, prospects, donors, and stakeholders. They understand and apply the principles of developing donor strategies. They will focus on providing the best\-in\-class donor stewardship and thrive on connecting and qualifying discovery prospects. They will be responsible for direct asks in support of global, regional, and local priorities to meet funding needs. They listen for opportunities for gifts of assets or other non\-cash gifts. They are energetic and focus on the daily work habits that are the foundations for success. They involve their Kentucky colleagues, Development Committee, Board of Trustees, and other natural partners, keeping them apprised of interactions, issues or concerns, and direct questions as appropriate. They can communicate the TNC programs in broad and compelling terms. The ADOP will be required to use TNC's Blackbaud donor database and participate in developing proposals. This position would require regular travel in Kentucky, focusing on particular geographies for donor meetings.


### **Responsibilities and Scope:**

* Manage a portfolio of 75\-100 donors, including donors who can give over $100,000 once the portfolio is developed. Raise an annual fundraising goal of $500,000 or more, in addition to a goal for bequest notifications and legacy gifts.
* Conduct 400 moves and interactions during a Fiscal Year (based on the assumption of a portfolio of 100 donors), with special attention to cultivating relationships with new prospects to the organization.
* Manage and implement strategic and highly curated events and/or donor engagement opportunities.
* Develop personalized written proposals and submit grant proposals, as needed.
* Act independently and in the supervisor’s stead as needed, and exercise independent judgment to identify and solve problems.
* Decisions may have a program\-wide impact and may bind the organization financially or legally.
* Demonstrate sensitivity in handling confidential information.
* Ensure compliance with TNC policies and procedures and external (donor/legal/IRS) requirements.
* Financial responsibility includes working within a budget and meeting fundraising objectives.
* Manage assigned projects, coordinating the work of peers and setting deadlines.
* May manage or participate in complex or sensitive negotiations.
* Convey the mission of TNC to diverse groups essential to the organization's overall prosperity.
* Travel (mostly within Kentucky) and work flexible hours as needed.
* Work environment involves only infrequent exposure to disagreeable elements and minor physical exertion and/or strain.
### **What You’ll Bring:**

* Bachelor's degree and 5 years of related experience or an equivalent combination.
* Experience building and maintaining long\-term relationships with fundraising constituents such as major donors, foundations, and corporations.
* Experience in asking for and closing gifts of $25,000 or more.
* Experience in managing and tracking multiple prospects and donors.
* Experience working with cross\-functional teams.
* Experience, coursework, or other training in fundraising principles and practices.

**Desired Qualifications:**

* Ability to determine an individual's interests, capacity, and potential for helping TNC meet its goals and act appropriately to tie those interests with our work.
* Ability to educate and inform prospective and existing donors about appropriate giving vehicles.
* Ability to implement and manage fundraising plans, including individualized cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship plans.
* Proven track record and experience with securing annual gifts, corporate philanthropy, campaigns, major gifts, and planned gifts.
* Experience in event planning and curated donor stewardship events.
* Demonstrated experience using listening, diplomacy, and tact to build strong relationships and motivate donors.
* Strong writing skills for grants and personalized proposal development
* Proven ability to negotiate sensitive philanthropic agreements.

This position requires a valid driver's license and compliance with **TNC’s Auto Safety Program** . Employees may not drive Conservancy\-owned/leased vehicles, rental cars, or personal vehicles on behalf of the Conservancy if considered "high risk drivers." To learn more about the Auto Safety Program, visit the Auto Safety information page . Employment in this position will be contingent upon com

**Location:** Albany, NY **Category:** Professional **Job Type:** Full\-time **Posted On:** Wed Jul 22 2026 **Job Description:**  

  

Since its founding in 1959, the Massry School of Business has been committed to providing high\-quality, high\-value business education to a diverse student population. As one of seven schools within the University at Albany \- one of four university centers in the State University of New York (SUNY) system \- the School serves approximately 2,284 undergraduate and 398 graduate students. Located in New York's Capital Region, the School is a recognized leader in business education and plays a vital role in advancing professional and management education regionally and globally.


The School's mission is grounded in expanding opportunity and advancing social mobility, preparing students from diverse backgrounds for meaningful careers and lifelong success. This commitment resonates deeply with alumni, corporate partners, and philanthropic supporters who are invested in shaping the next generation of business leaders.


The Massry School is experiencing significant philanthropic momentum. The current Inspire the Next campaign has surpassed its $30 million goal ahead of schedule, strengthening support for scholarships, faculty excellence, student success initiatives, and strategic priorities. Building on this success, the School is well positioned for continued growth in philanthropy and engagement.


The University at Albany seeks an accomplished and enterprising fundraising professional to serve as Senior Director of Development for the Massry School of Business. The senior director will be responsible for engaging high net\-worth alumni and friends and designing and executing solicitation strategies to secure gifts of $25,000\-$10,000,000\+.


The senior director will help provide the vision and leadership for fundraising efforts for the school's highest strategic priorities. This individual will collaborate with campus partners, in particular the dean, and develop coordinated strategies aimed at maximizing the lifetime engagement of a portfolio that includes many of UAlbany's most affluent and important prospects.


**Primary Responsibilities:**

ResCare Community Living, a part of the Sevita family, provides quality services that empower individuals, enhance independence, and help people live well. Since 1974, we’ve supported individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities to live more independently at home and in their community. With our dedicated team and experienced staff, we provide person\-centered services that help people build skills, overcome challenges, and reach their full potential.

**SUMMARY**
===========


* Position Type: Full\-time, exempt, salaried supervisor role.
* Scope of Role:
	+ Responsible for managing the services delivered to a designated program or set of programs, and the operation of programs.

	+ Oversee organization and implementation of direct supports, person\-centered planning and implementation, health care, advocacy, stakeholder relations, regulatory compliance, staffing, training, financial management, and maintenance of the physical environment.
  


**ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS**
*To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to satisfactorily perform each essential function listed below.*  


**Staff Management:**
=====================

* Recruitment \& Hiring: May participate in hiring events as needed and partner with Talent Acquisition to identify hiring needs for Direct Support Professionals and Program Supervisors in consultation with the Area Director.


* Meetings \& Training: Organizes and implements on\-site orientation and training for assigned programs and monitors training compliance and documentation; conducts monthly staff meetings; attends monthly regional meetings and additional meetings as needed.


* Employee Scheduling: Designs and monitors schedules to ensure that all shifts are covered; monitors and minimizes overtime.


* Performance Evaluation: Conducts rotating performance snapshots and evaluations for direct reports in consultation with the PD’s manager; reviews and approves Direct Support Professional and other staff member’s performance evaluations.


* Coaching, Discipline and Termination: Coaches employees, correcting or directing employee behavior where required; may remove employees at their sole discretion in the event of emergency; may recommend and participate in decisions regarding discipline and termination in consultation with the PD’s manager and Human Resources; manages the termination of Direct Support Professionals or other staff members, as necessary.


* Employee Safety: Responsible for employee safety and workers’ compensation programs; monitors compliance; and assists in returning employees to work; implements, oversees, and trains employees about safety.
  


**Program Management:**
=======================


* Program Planning: Acts as the QIDP (if required), writes and approves individual program plans; trains and assists appropriate staff members with implementation of plans across all shifts.


* Records: Periodically audits individual records and other program documentation for accuracy and completion.


* Program Team Meetings: Conducts progress meetings and (quarterly, annual, and/or other) reviews as appropriate or determined by the interdisciplinary team; provides and approves written and progress reviews.


* Compliance: Oversees licensing compliance for program services and supports; ensures timely and accurate program documentation; implements the Sevita compliance plan and manages individual rights implementation while maintaining confidentiality and adhering to individual rights standards.


* Management of Individuals’ Assets: Implements system and oversees management of individual’s funds and property, assuring safety and accuracy; audits, monitors, and reviews individual’s financial accounts.


* Program Stakeholder Relations: Maintains productive and professional relations with individuals, families, case managers and other stakeholders; provides regular communication to stakeholders; and acts as point of contact for all stakeholders; implements satisfaction improvement plans.


* Government Relations: Engages in government and community relations; participates in advocacy efforts and ensures preparedness for home visits (e.g., staff, families, legislators).


* Incidents: Implements proactive strategies to reduce or eliminate serious incidents; takes immediate steps to correct potential safety concerns and limit the potential for continued concern or incident; reviews all incidents and directs appropriate response; ensures timely incident reporting; and signs and submits all incident reports as required.
  


**Health Care:**
================


* Appointments: Oversees coordination of health care appointments and monitors individual attendance.


* Medications: Oversees system for medication administration, documentation, and security; reviews documentation for accuracy and completeness; ensures all applicable employees are certified or trained in medication administration.


* Medical Supplies and Equipment: Approves purchases of medical supplies and equipment; and ensures training in proper use to all employees.


* Dietary planning: Monitors food service in accordance with dietary plans and to meet regulatory compliance; ensures consideration for special needs, occasions, and individual choice; ensures all applicable employees are certified or trained in meal preparation and/or dietary planning.
  


**Financial Management**:
=========================


* Purchasing: Approves purchase of food, supplies and equipment; and reviews and monitors monthly invoices for accuracy and budgetary compliance; attests to company credit card statements and monthly invoices in accordance with company policy.


* Billing and utilization: Oversees census, attendance information or other required documentation to assure accurate billing; ensures program documentation is accurate, complete, and compliant; monitors utilization between authorization and provision of services and supports.

ATEC Executive Director (4872U) \#87651 \- Vice Chancellor for Research Immediate Office About Berkeley
  
  

At the University of California, Berkeley , we are dedicated to fostering a community where everyone feels welcome and can thrive. Our culture of openness, freedom, and belonging makes it a special place for students, faculty, and staff, who are among the most talented and accomplished anywhere, including Nobel laureates, Pulitzer Prize winners, and MacArthur Fellows. Since our founding in 1868, we have been an engine for innovation \- driving intellectual, economic, and social progress that benefits California, the U.S., and beyond. Together, we change the world.
  
  

At Berkeley, the best careers are built on a foundation of continuous learning and growth . We actively support professional development by providing all full\-time staff employees with at least 80 hours of paid time per year and provide space for supportive colleague communities via numerous employee resource groups.
  
  

We invite applicants who are inspired by our Principles of Community and who are eager to be part of our exciting Strategic Plan , which charts Berkeley's next era of excellence.
  
  

Learn more about some of our accomplishments at Points of Pride .
  
  

Departmental Overview
  
  

To support the creation and commercialization of new ventures grounded in academic research in advanced technologies, UC Berkeley is establishing the Advanced Technologies Entrepreneurship Center (ATEC). Operating within the Office of the Chief Innovation and Entrepreneurship Officer and administered by the Vice Chancellor for Research, ATEC serves as the operational centerpiece of the broader Academic Innovation Catalyst (AIC) initiative.
  
  

ATEC incorporates elements of a "venture studio" model, providing ready\-to\-launch academic innovators with deep, focused, hands\-on support including business and legal advice, talent matchmaking, team building, and G\&A support. Grounded in deep academic deep\-tech research (excluding life sciences), ATEC helps launch discoveries across materials science, chemistry, physics, data science, environmental/climate science, and energy into the world for positive impact.
  
  

Position Summary
  
  

The founding Executive Director (ED) of the Advanced Technologies Entrepreneurship Center (ATEC) is a highly entrepreneurial leader responsible for building, launching, and leading all facets of ATEC. Sitting structurally between early\-stage proof\-of\-concept (POC) grant programs and first institutional venture capital rounds, the Executive Director guides academic innovators through intensive team building, customer discovery, and venture creation.
  
  

Application Review Date
  
  

**The First Review Date for this job is:** August 4, 2026\. The department's application review will commence on August 11, 2026\.
  
  

Responsibilities
  
  

Venture Studio Strategy, Launch, \& Core Operations \- 50%
  
  

**Program Architecture:** Develop the specific contours, operational frameworks, and benchmarks of the ATEC venture studio program to drive research\-grounded deep tech company formations, advancing UC Berkeley's strategic priorities in the area.
  
**Strategic Leadership:** Applies high\-level expertise in strategic business development to identify joint venture, affiliation and other partnership opportunities that support ATEC's strategic priorities, and recommends innovative approaches to achieving strategic goals.
  
**Cohort Management:** Provide hands\-on mentorship and "player\-coach" and strategic business development guidance to cohort founders, shepherding up to two new core company formations annually (with potential to scale).
  
**Talent Recruitment:** Recruit, onboard, and manage three Venture Fellows (performing landscape analyses and other support tasks) and up to six Entrepreneurs\-in\-Residence (EIRs) per year to build balanced founding teams around academic PIs.
  
**Venture Services:** Procure and oversee essential "non\-core" operational infrastructure for cohort companies, including finance, payroll, legal counsel, and General and Administrative (G\&A) services.
  
**Financial Oversight:** Administer ATEC's annual operating budget of $650,000 and track key operational performance metrics (e.g., candidate pipelines, company formations, follow\-on VC financing amounts), and provide strategic advice about deployment and co\-management of up to $2\.1M per year in proof\-of\-concept grant funding and investment in grant awardees.
  
Inter\-Campus and Inter\-Division Program Synchronization \& Stewardship \- 20%
  
  

**Post\-Award Mentorship:** Applies expert knowledge of business development practices to lead post\-award mentorship and operational stewardship for AIC POC grant awardees across the Berkeley campus, including with College of Computing, Data Science and Society (CDSS), and all four CITRIS campuses (UC Berkeley, UC Davis, UC Merced, and UC Santa Cruz), as well as supporting the Bakar\-AIC POC grant awardees, leveraging ATEC's EIR pool and other resources.
  
**Recruitment \& Outreach:** Coordinate with the College of Computing, Data Science and Society (CDSS), CITRIS and the Bakar ecosystem's communications teams to drive unified marketing, active pipeline recruitment, and entrepreneurial program outreach.
  
**Selection Committees:** Source and deliver 3\-5 external selection committee members (such as SkyDeck mentors), coordinating committee composition directly with CITRIS and CDSS leadership.
  
**Progress Calibration:** Convene and lead quarterly internal progress review sessions with stakeholders to evaluate awardee translation metrics and program integration.
  
**Donor Coordination:** Author donor\-facing progress updates for awardees, working in tandem with development staff to secure multi\-year donor expectations.
  
Ecosystem Partnerships, Capital, \& Technology Licensing \- 20%

Description:
**ORGANIZATIONAL OVERVIEW**


STRIVE is a national leader in workforce development solutions, empowering individuals to discover their best future selves through innovative, evidence\-based career training and holistic, lifelong support. STRIVE partners with employers, funders and community organizations to close gaps in workforce needs and help local economies thrive. Founded in East Harlem, NY, in 1984, STRIVE has grown from its roots to now offer programs in locations across the country.


For more than 40 years, STRIVE has been a catalyst for economic change and prosperity in underserved communities. Our tuition\-free skills and job readiness training, one\-on\-one support and lifetime personal coaching prepare students for careers in high\-growth industries such as healthcare, construction and logistics. With a proven pathway to possible, 80% of STRIVE students graduate, 76% attain employment and 70% retain employment long\-term.


Looking to the future, STRIVE has embarked on an ambitious ten\-year growth plan to increase its impact in more locations nationwide, aiming to serve up to 10,000 individuals each year by 2033\.


We meet individuals where they are, providing free programs tailored to adults 18\+, including those impacted by the justice system. At STRIVE, there is always a path forward.


See why STRIVE has been the pathway to possible for more than 40 years in our 3\-minute mission video.

**POSITION SUMMARY**


STRIVE seeks a dynamic, strategic, entrepreneurial leader who will be responsible for overseeing the launch and expansion of STRIVE Houston, located in Houston, Texas.


Supported by STRIVE’s National Program Team, the Executive Director will join a network of STRIVE sites delivering our dynamic programs, including our flagship Career Path program and specialized justice\-impacted programs, across the nation. To be successful in this role, the ideal candidate will thrive in a fast\-paced environment where the ability to manage multiple relationships and a range of small and large\-scale projects at once is vital. The mandate for the STRIVE Houston Executive Director is to establish STRIVE as a preferred partner for middle skills jobs in the region and to expand the number of job seekers served.


The successful candidate will be outcomes focused, highly motivated, growth\-oriented and experienced, who can work collaboratively with leaders across the agency to meet the organization’s goals. They will have substantial experience in building and executing new programs, fundraising, management, operations, and a deep understanding of cultivating and managing finances from various revenue sources.


The founding Executive Director leads program execution, community engagement, partner cultivation and facility establishment to achieve success in our Houston location. They will provide vision and leadership, fiscal responsibility and sustainability, budget oversight, and program management. They will work with STRIVE’s national Development team to execute our Houston fundraising strategy, building the local funder relationships and being an active partner in fulfilling their revenue goals. As STRIVE Houston grows, it will look to raise upwards of $2 million annually from local and national sources.


They will lead and develop a team to execute and advance our evidence\-driven training model in both our career path and reentry\-focused programs. They will work in close partnership with our Regional Executive Director and the National Program \& Impact team to implement our programs and ensure their continuing success. At STRIVE, we operate in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment. The Executive Director will have the opportunity to shape an innovative workforce development approach in Houston, as well as further build and establish the STRIVE reputation throughout the Houston workforce development and justice\-aligned community.

**Fundraising \& Community Engagement**

* Develop and lead STRIVE Houston’s local fundraising strategy in partnership with the national Development team.
* Serve as the “face of STRIVE” in Houston, representing STRIVE in regional meetings, conferences, panels, and media opportunities.
* With the Development team, prospect, cultivate, and generate local operating revenue through a diverse range of fundraising activities targeted at individual, institutional, and public sources.
* Oversee execution of outcomes and fulfill requirements for local grants and contracts.

* Develop and lead the implementation of STRIVE program(s) in Houston with the support of STRIVE’s National Program Team.

* Develop and implement new training initiatives in response to local demand.
* Cultivate a pipeline of employers in Houston who are willing to invest in training design and implementation, and who will commit to hiring STRIVE graduates.
* Increase awareness of STRIVE’s program to targeted communities.
* Expand enrollment in STRIVE’s training.
* Develop strategic alliances with other local service providers, local and state education and human services agencies, community colleges, and other aligned organizations to expand STRIVE’s implementation and presence.

**Team Leadership**

The role is sharply focused on moving elite prospects toward transformative commitments in support of the University's comprehensive capital campaign and strategic priorities.


Reporting directly to the Vice President for University Advancement (VPUA), with dotted line supervisory responsibilities to the Director, Capital Campaign. The PGO operates with a dual primary mandate:


* Supporting the VPUA and the President on the institution's top, high\-level institutional donor relationships.
* Directly managing a highly focused, elite portfolio of top\-capacity prospects capable of making significant $1M\+ principal gifts.


The PGO helps build and sustain a robust, top\-tier pipeline of leadership\-level philanthropic support while driving sophisticated, donor\-centered campaign success. Continued funding for the position is contingent upon the availability of campaign funding.

* (E) Academic \& Leadership Alignment: Partner with the Executive Director of Development and Alumni Relations, Deans, and faculty to identify critical institutional funding priorities and convert them into compelling, high\-level philanthropic investment concepts.

**3\. Strategic Stewardship \& Administrative Integrity (10%)**


(These are supporting functions focused strictly on the principal/major gift level; routine execution, event logistics, and broad reporting are handled by supporting staff).

new_jobs.title: Director of Development
new_jobs.description:
**About Friends of Waterfront Park**

  

Friends of Waterfront Park (Friends), a nonprofit organization, was established in 2012\. City officials and civic leaders recognized that, like all great parks in cities across the country, Waterfront Park needed a nonprofit leader to ensure its lasting success. From our early days of facilitating public engagement around the design of Waterfront Park to our historic fundraising Campaign for Waterfront Park and our development of public programs and public safety strategies centered in compassion, Friends has been a key partner to the City of Seattle and communities across the city in an unprecedented effort to build an extraordinary urban public space.

  

In 2025, the Waterfront Park Grand Opening Celebration brought more than 50,000 people to celebrate a new era for Seattle’s central waterfront. With the $170M Campaign complete and Waterfront Park open to the community, Friends is focused on welcoming everyone to this iconic Seattle gem all year through fundraising, programming, public safety investments, park experience, and long\-term stewardship.

 **Our Mission** affirms why we’re here: to bring people and local communities together to ensure Waterfront Park thrives as a vibrant gathering space that fosters connections, and honor Seattle’s natural beauty and stories.

 **Our Vision** imagines where we’re headed. Seattle is transformed by Waterfront Park, a public space sparking joy, connection, and belonging with every visit.

 **Our Values** of Community, Equity, Care, Collaboration, and Adaptability ground how we work, from programming and partnerships to public safety and storytelling.

  

Friends of Waterfront Park recognizes and acknowledges historic and existing systemic racism embedded in our city. Friends commits to being an anti\-racist organization by prioritizing racial equity within the organization and in the public spaces we operate.

 **Land Acknowledgement**

  

Waterfront Park is situated on the land of the Coast Salish Peoples, who have resided here since time immemorial and continue to thrive. With respect and humility, we acknowledge the history of the waterfront, the dispossession of land from the Coast Salish people and, most importantly, the strength and resilience of Native people and their culture through this history and to the present.

 **Position Summary**

  

Friends of Waterfront Park is seeking a strategic, relationship\-driven **Director of Development** to lead and grow its philanthropy revenue program. Reporting to the CEO, the Director of Development will oversee and grow a **$3\.66M annual fundraising portfolio** and lead a philanthropy team spanning major gifts, leadership giving, events, sponsorships, grants, annual giving, and database functions. Serving as a member of the Senior Leadership Team, the Director contributes to organizational strategy, alignment, and culture while advancing Friends’ philanthropic vision. This is a role for an experienced fundraiser who brings both the relationship skills to personally manage major donor portfolios and to develop and execute ongoing donor cultivation and stewardship strategies. They also bring the management breadth to provide strategic oversight of a full function development team. They are energized by the challenge of building systems, developing people, and stewarding major donor relationships simultaneously.

 **This is a full\-time, exempt, hybrid role based in Seattle, WA.**

 **The Opportunity**

  

Friends of Waterfront Park is a growing organization with a total operating budget of $9\.3M, now entering an exciting new chapter following the completion of its historic $170M capital campaign.



The Director of Development will lead an experienced philanthropy team through a significant transition: modernizing development operations and data systems, building out a comprehensive annual giving and grants program, refining the events strategy, and stewarding the major donor relationships that are foundational to Friends' long\-term success.

  

The Director will have meaningful visibility with the Board of Directors and the Development Committee, working in close partnership with the CEO to align fundraising strategy with organizational priorities. Friends' signature fundraising events, the Pier Party and Dinner with Friends, anchor the summer calendar and represent significant donor engagement opportunities the Director will help shape.

 **The Fundraising Portfolio**

  

The Director of Development will lead strategy and relationship management across Friends’ fundraising program. While the 2027 portfolio may adjust in scope, the 2026 portfolio totals approximately $3\.66M in annual fundraising and includes:

  

* Leadership Circle: $1,200,000 (33%)
* Fundraising Events (Key events include Pier Party, Dinner with Friends): $990,000 (27%)
* Major Gifts: $700,000 (19%)
* New Grants / Institutional Funding: $600,000 (16%)
* Annual Giving and Board Giving: $172,500 (5%)

  



The Director will personally manage a portfolio of approximately 100 top donors and prospects. The top 25 relationships will be co\-managed in close coordination with the CEO and Board; the remaining 75 donors and prospects will sit in the Director's independent portfolio. The Director will also staff and support the Development Committee.

  

In addition, the Director will provide stewardship oversight for previous campaign contributions ($2,000,000\) and secured grants ($1,853,238\) currently in the budget. Candidates should be prepared to engage with donors and funders across the full scope of Friends’ philanthropy revenue.

  

**Responsibilities**

 **Fundraising Strategy and Leadership**

  

* Lead and grow Friends’ philanthropy revenue program across major gifts, leadership giving, annual fund, institutional funding, and events.
* Develop a multiyear fundraising strategy that transitions Friends from capital campaign mode to sustained operational philanthropy.
*

**About Green Attic**

Green Attic is a growing home\-services company serving residential and commercial customers throughout the Chicagoland area.

Our operations include roofing, insulation, energy\-efficiency improvements, remodeling, restoration, and multi\-trade construction projects.

Ownership has built a strong sales pipeline and revenue engine. We are now hiring an experienced Chief Operating Officer to lead company\-wide execution, strengthen management, improve profitability, and build the systems required for continued growth.

This is not primarily a field\-supervisor or production\-manager position. The COO must understand construction operations deeply, but the main responsibility is to lead the business at an executive level.

The right person will translate company goals into operating plans, develop department leaders, manage financial and operational performance, and ensure the organization executes without depending on ownership for routine decisions.

**Position Overview**

The Chief Operating Officer will oversee day\-to\-day business operations across production, project management, scheduling, customer service, warehouse, purchasing, administration, and field execution.

This leader will work directly with ownership to establish priorities, allocate resources, improve organizational structure, and maintain accountability across departments.

The COO must have significant construction or home\-services experience and understand how projects move from sales and estimating through scheduling, production, billing, completion, warranty, and customer closeout.

This position requires executive judgment, financial discipline, leadership maturity, and the ability to build a scalable operating system across multiple brands and service divisions.

* Improve communication and coordination between sales, production, project management, warehouse, accounting, and customer service.
* Identify leadership gaps and recruit, train, coach, or replace team members when required.
* Establish performance expectations and hold managers accountable for results, not activity or excuses.
* Lead major operational decisions involving staffing, capacity, scheduling, subcontractors, equipment, materials, vendors, and expansion.
* Reduce operational dependence on ownership by creating reliable leaders, systems, and escalation procedures.
* Evaluate new services, markets, technologies, and operational investments based on profitability and execution capacity.
* Protect the company from operational, safety, compliance, customer\-service, and financial risk.

**Construction and Production Oversight**

The COO will not personally manage every project or crew. However, this person must understand construction well enough to challenge managers, identify weak planning, evaluate risk, and determine whether operations are being run correctly.

Responsibilities include:

* Identify recurring field problems and require permanent process corrections rather than repeated emergency fixes.

**Financial and Performance Management**

* Partner with ownership and accounting to establish budgets, forecasts, labor targets, and margin expectations.
* Review project\-level and departmental financial performance.

**Director of Advancement**

**About Us** 

Food Share of Lincoln County’s mission is to alleviate hunger in Lincoln County by distributing healthy, nutritious food to our network of agencies and by empowering our community through education. The Food Share warehouse serves as the central distribution point for 23 partner agencies in the county. As a regional food bank, FSLC is overseen by a Board of Directors and collaborates with Oregon Food Bank to distribute USDA commodity foods, food donated by producers in the food industry, and local donations from retailers and individuals.

FSLC has been in existence since September of 1982\. Beyond our longevity, we have strong community goodwill and support, which are significant. Community members make up our volunteers, donors, partner agencies, board of directors, and a variety of businesses and organizations that support our work.

We also are an example of good stewardship and strategy. Our current warehouse/office space is owned outright and we have completed the purchase of our future warehouse location this past year.

FSLC focuses on serving those in the community with respect and dignity and those who are vulnerable; low\-income, working\-class families, military veterans, BIPOC, LGBQT, foster youth, seniors, and unhoused. We partner with fellow non\-profit agencies and housing units to develop programming that requires the fewest obstacles and affords easy access to those in need. We also engage and empower members by offering Nutrition Education regarding fresh foods and healthy eating.

**Position Summary**

The **Director of Advancement** will participate in Food Share’s fundraising and community engagement efforts, focusing on donor relations, events, communications, capital projects, and providing administrative support. This role is ideal for someone who is highly organized, detail\-oriented, and passionate about building community support to address hunger.

**Key Responsibilities**

* **Fundraising \& Donor Relations 50%**
* Maintain donor database; process and acknowledge gifts promptly.
* Support and develop annual giving campaigns, donor stewardship, estate gifts, and cultivation activities.
* Utilize data informed strategies.
* Grant writing, tracking and reporting.
* **Events \& Campaigns 30%**
* Help plan and execute fundraising events and community engagement activities.
* Coordinate logistics, board and volunteer engagement, and follow\-up.
* Assist with capital campaign and other fundraising initiatives.
* **Communications \& Outreach 10%**
* Develop donor\-centered communications, including newsletters, social media posts.
* Share stories of impact and increase visibility of Food Share’s programs.
* **Administrative Support 10%**
* Provide admin and support as needed.

**Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in communications, nonprofit management, or related field; or equivalent work experience.
* 2 \- 3 years of nonprofit development, communications, or administrative experience preferred.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills.
* Knowledge using donor management software (e.g., Little Green Light, DonorPerfect, or similar).
* Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite, Canva, and social media platforms.
* Highly organized, detail\-oriented, and able to manage multiple priorities.
* Passion for hunger relief and commitment to Food Share’s mission.

**Compensation \& Benefits**

* Salary: $24\.00 \- $26\.00 per hour \- Paid health, vision, dental plan. After one year, matching up to 3% retirement plan.
* Benefits include health insurance, paid vacation, sick leave, and a retirement plan.
* Professional development opportunities.

Throughout its history, CPH has launched groundbreaking new work, produced celebrated classics and contemporary plays, and served as a vital artistic home for generations of artists and audiences.

  



As Cleveland Play House enters a transformative new chapter, the organization is building on its legacy while embracing new opportunities for artistic excellence, community impact, and long\-term sustainability. The Director of Development will play a critical leadership role in shaping that future.

Reporting directly to the Managing Director and serving as a member of the senior leadership team, the Director of Development will cultivate meaningful relationships with donors, foundations, corporations, and community partners while developing strategies to increase contributed revenue and deepen philanthropic engagement.

* Strengthen donor retention, renewal, and upgrade strategies.



Board and Volunteer Engagement


* Partner with the Managing Director and Board leadership to advance fundraising efforts.
* Support Development Committee activities and fundraising initiatives.
* Assist trustees in identifying, cultivating, and soliciting prospective donors.
* + Help foster a culture of philanthropy throughout the organization.



Team Leadership

* Establish clear goals, priorities, and accountability measures.
* Foster a collaborative, donor\-centered, and mission\-driven culture.

* Strong relationship\-building and donor cultivation skills.
* Excellent written, verbal, and presentation abilities.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively in a fast\-paced environment.
* Strong organizational and project management skills.
* Ability to work evenings and weekends as required for donor events, performances, and organizational activities.



Preferred Qualifications


* Seven or more years of fundraising leadership experience.
* Experience in performing arts, higher education, cultural, or nonprofit organizations.
* Experience with campaign planning and execution.
* Knowledge of Cleveland’s philanthropic community.
* Experience managing fundraising staff and volunteers.

The Development Director for Catholic Schools provides strategic leadership and support to advance development efforts across Catholic schools in the Diocese of Charlotte. Skills
